title: senecio triangularis
intro: Senecio triangularis, also known as Jacob's ladder or fireweed, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a natural antiseptic. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as treating respiratory issues, coughs, and skin irritations, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea, tincture, or salve, and can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
